test.command: set -e; ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then (cd "$ROOT/apps/mobile" && flutter test --concurrency=5); fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo test --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --jobs 5; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild test -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS'; fi

typecheck.command: ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then flutter analyze "$ROOT/apps/mobile"; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo check --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --all-targets; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS' build >/dev/null; fi

lint.command: set -e; ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then flutter analyze "$ROOT/apps/mobile"; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo fmt --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --all --check && cargo clippy --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS' build >/dev/null; fi

blockingIssues.description: The mobile client subscribes only to the currently open thread's websocket stream, so off-screen thread activity never updates list/status UI as required by VAL-THREAD-011.
Mobile secure storage is still backed by InMemorySecureStore, so trusted-session, selected-thread, and cached-thread state do not survive real app process death or relaunch.
Thread live-stream disconnect handling marks the UI stale/offline but never schedules automatic reconnect/catch-up, leaving recovery dependent on a manual Retry tap.
Live approval_requested events update the timeline only; the approvals queue and thread approval surfaces do not refresh automatically, so newly gated actions can stay invisible until manual refresh.
Branch-switch approvals do not expose the exact target branch under review in the mobile detail UI, missing the exact decision context required by VAL-APPROVAL-002.
Late approval resolution conflicts such as approval_not_pending leave queue/thread state stale as pending until manual refresh, breaking approval status synchronization.
Git mutation buttons can remain enabled when git status failed to load even though the controller refuses those mutations without resolved git status, violating the required safe disabled state.
Notification preference toggles are only persisted locally and affect UI chrome, not actual approval/live-activity notification delivery or suppression, so VAL-SETTINGS-004 is not met.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that the current mobile secure-store provider is still backed by InMemorySecureStore, so app relaunch does not yet persist trusted-session or cached-thread state.
Documented that approval records currently expose generic git target identifiers instead of concrete branch/remote destination values.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how mobile workers should handle manual verification in headless sessions: either explicitly permit widget/integration-test-based fallback evidence when no simulator/device session is feasible, or require workers to record the missing manual check as a deviation instead of marking followedProcedure true.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for mobile-thread-list-and-search, mobile-approvals-queue-and-resolution, mobile-git-controls-and-context, and mobile-settings-access-modes-and-unpair all flagged that the current skill mandates manual local-target verification, while headless worker handoffs either used widget-test fallback or omitted interactive evidence entirely.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax the strict 'write failing tests first' instruction for cross-layer Flutter slices so workers may add the minimal interface/controller scaffolding needed before the first meaningful failing assertions when test doubles or contracts do not yet exist.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for mobile-thread-detail-live-and-timeline and mobile-composer-and-turn-controls both recorded justified deviations because the current skill wording required failing tests before the bridge/controller/page contract existed well enough to compile realistic widget scenarios.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated manual-verification skill observations from multiple mobile reviews.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of the single systemic mobile-worker guidance recommendation captured in suggestedGuidanceUpdates.

rejectedObservations.observation: The composer-controls review repeated the scaffold-before-tests concern already captured by the thread-detail review.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of the single systemic mobile-worker test-order recommendation captured in suggestedGuidanceUpdates.
